array-header.comment: TOON v3.3 array header `key[N]{fields}:` — key optional at root, delimiter symbol optional inside the bracket ([N|], [N<TAB>]), field list optional. Not anchored to line start so it also matches after a `- ` list-item prefix. TOON quoting rules keep `[]{}:` out of unquoted text, so a mid-line match can only be a real header.

keyed-map-header.comment: reddb-io Extension 2 (keyed-map collapse): `key{fields}:` with no [N] segment. The field list may open with the delimiter symbol, e.g. `map{|id|name}:`.

field-list.comment: Field list between braces. Recursive groups (Extension 1/4: `customer{name,country}`), primitive-list columns (Extension 3: `tags[;]`), fixed-width cells (Extension 4: `values[3|]`), and dotted paths (Extension 5: `issue.number`, `labels.0`) all reduce to these token shapes.

key-value.comment: `key: value` and bare `key:` (opens a nested object). Also matches keyed-map row lines `mapKey: cells` (Extension 2), whose value region is tokenized per cell.

cycle.comment: reddb-io Extension 5 (cyclic discriminated arrays): the complete-cycle RLE expression `cycle(label,label,...)*repeats` carried by the `order:` metadata field.

list-item.comment: Expanded-list item prefix `- `; a bare `-` is an empty object list item.

string.comment: Double-quoted string with the closed v3.3 escape repertoire; any other escape is invalid. Strings never span lines.
string.name: string.quoted.double.toon

empty-array.comment: The literal empty array `[]`, at root or as a field value (`items: []`).

number.comment: Canonical decimal plus the exponent forms decoders accept. Leading-zero tokens like `05` are deliberately excluded — they decode as strings.
number.name: constant.numeric.toon

delimiter.comment: Cell separators: comma, pipe, tab (the three active delimiters) and `;` (Extension 3 in-cell list sub-delimiter).
delimiter.name: punctuation.separator.sequence.toon

unquoted-cell.comment: Fallback: an unquoted string cell/value. Runs last, so headers, keys, constants and numbers win at the same position.
unquoted-cell.name: string.unquoted.toon
